docs.title: CRM Order Management API
docs.text:
Welcome to the CRM Order Management API documentation portal. This documentation portal demonstrates a Docs-as-Code workflow used by technical writers to build scalable, developer-friendly API documentation systems. Every documentation update is automatically validated, built, and deployed via GitHub Actions CI/CD.

The CRM Order Management API enables developers to manage the full lifecycle of customer orders from creation to delivery within a CRM system.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Order Management API is a RESTful API that follows OpenAPI 3.0 standards. It provides endpoints for creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting customer orders.

Property Value Version 1.0.0 Protocol HTTPS Format JSON Auth API Key (X-API-Key header) Base URL https://api.example.com/v1

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
• Create orders – Submit a new customer order with product and quantity details.
  • List orders – Retrieve a paginated list of all orders, with optional status filtering.
  • Get an order – Retrieve full details of a specific order by its ID.
  • Update an order – Modify order fields such as status, quantity, or price.
  • Delete an order – Permanently remove an order from the system.

docs.title: Order Lifecycle
docs.text:
Orders follow a structured status progression:

CREATED → PROCESSING → SHIPPED → DELIVERED
                    ↘ CANCELLED

The following table describes each status.

Status Description CREATED Order has been placed and is awaiting processing PROCESSING Order is being prepared for shipment SHIPPED Order has been dispatched to the customer DELIVERED Order has been received by the customer CANCELLED Order has been cancelled

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text: Resource Link Interactive API Reference Swagger UI Getting Started Getting Started Authentication Guide Authentication Full API Reference CRM Order Management API Error Handling Error Handling

docs.title: API Versioning Strategy
docs.text: This document outlines the versioning strategy for the CRM Order Management API.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The API follows Semantic Versioning (SemVer) format: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH

  • MAJOR: Breaking changes to the API contract
  • MINOR: New backwards-compatible features
  • PATCH: Bug fixes and non-breaking improvements

Current Version: 1.0.0

docs.title: Versioning in the URL
docs.text:
The API version is included in the request URL:

https://api.example.com/v1/orders
                       ^^
                    Version 1

This allows multiple API versions to coexist, enabling smooth migrations for API consumers.

docs.title: What Constitutes a Breaking Change?
docs.text:
A breaking change requires a new MAJOR version:

  • Removing an endpoint
  • Removing a required field from a response
  • Changing the type of a field (string → integer)
  • Changing the format of a field value
  • Renaming an endpoint or field
  • Changing HTTP method for an endpoint (e.g., GET → POST)
  • Changing authentication mechanism
  • Narrowing valid value ranges (e.g., max requests/minute: 100 → 50)

docs.title: What is Backwards Compatible?
docs.text:
A backwards-compatible change increments MINOR:

  • Adding a new optional field to a response (consumers ignore unknown fields)
  • Adding a new optional query parameter
  • Adding a new endpoint
  • Adding new enum values to a status field
  • Loosening constraints (e.g., max requests/minute: 100 → 150)
  • Improving error messages (same error code, clearer message)

docs.title: Deprecation Policy
docs.text:
Before removing functionality, the API will undergo a deprecation period:

  1. Announce the deprecation (in changelog, release notes)
  2. Warn API clients with Deprecation and Sunset HTTP headers
  3. Wait 6 months minimum before removing the deprecated feature
  4. Remove in the next MAJOR version

docs.title: Example Deprecation Headers
docs.text:
Deprecation: true
Sunset: Fri, 14 Apr 2027 00:00:00 GMT

docs.title: Step 1: Plan the Release
docs.text:
Review changes since the last release and determine the version bump:

Current: 1.0.0
New features added? → MINOR (1.1.0)
Only bugfixes? → PATCH (1.0.1)
Breaking changes? → MAJOR (2.0.0)

docs.title: Step 2: Update Documentation
docs.text:
1. Update docs/changelog.md:

    ## [1.1.0] – YYYY-MM-DD

### Added
- New `/orders/export` endpoint for bulk order export

### Changed
- Improved error messages for invalid status transitions

### Fixed
- Pagination limit validation off by one error

  2. Update version in docs/openapi/v1/openapi.yaml:

    info:
    version: 1.1.0

  3. Update README.md API Overview if endpoints changed

docs.title: Step 3: Test Locally
docs.text:
# Generate fresh docs
python scripts/generate_docs.py

# Build the site
mkdocs build

# Run validations
spectral lint docs/openapi/v1/openapi.yaml --ruleset .spectral.yaml

docs.title: Step 4: Create Release Tag
docs.text:
git tag -a v1.1.0 -m "Release v1.1.0: Add export endpoint"
git push origin v1.1.0

GitHub will automatically create a release from the tag.

docs.title: Step 5: CI/CD Deploy
docs.text:
• GitHub Actions pipeline runs automatically
  • OpenAPI spec is validated
  • Docs are generated and built
  • Site is deployed to GitHub Pages

docs.title: Running Multiple Versions
docs.text:
This repository currently maintains a single API version (v1). If future MAJOR versions are released:

docs/
├── openapi/
│   ├── v1/
│   │   └── openapi.yaml    (original)
│   ├── v2/
│   │   └── openapi.yaml    (next major version)
│   └── INDEX.md            (version guide)
├── index.md                (links to latest docs)
├── getting-started-v1.md   (guide for v1 users)
└── getting-started-v2.md   (guide for v2 users)

Each version would have:

  • Separate OpenAPI spec
  • Version-specific documentation
  • Separate Swagger UI instance
  • Backward compatibility notes

docs.title: For Each Release
docs.text:
1. GitHub Release Notes – What changed, why, migration instructions
  2. Changelog – Detailed list of all changes
  3. Blog post (future) – Major features and improvements
  4. Email notification (future) – Alert registered API users

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: The CRM Order Management API uses API key authentication. Every request must include a valid API key in the request header.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. You receive an API key from the API team.
  2. Include the key in the X-API-Key header of every HTTP request.
  3. The server validates the key before processing the request.

If the key is missing or invalid, the API returns a 401 Unauthorized response.

docs.title: Obtaining an API Key
docs.text: Contact the support team to request a key:

docs.title: Including the API Key in Requests
docs.text:
Pass your API key in the X-API-Key request header:

curl -X GET "https://api.example.com/v1/orders" \
  -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key-here"

docs.title: Postman
docs.text:
1. Open your request in Postman.
  2. Go to the Headers tab.

  3. Add a new header:

    • Key: X-API-Key
    • Value: your-api-key-here

docs.title: Python (requests library)
docs.text:
import requests

headers = {
    "X-API-Key": "your-api-key-here",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

response = requests.get("https://api.example.com/v1/orders", headers=headers)
print(response.json())

docs.title: JavaScript (fetch)
docs.text:
const response = await fetch("https://api.example.com/v1/orders", {
  headers: {
    "X-API-Key": "your-api-key-here",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
  }
});
const data = await response.json();
console.log(data);

docs.title: Authentication Errors
Security Best Practices
docs.text:
• Never expose your API key in client-side code, public repositories, or logs.

  • Store keys in environment variables or a secrets manager (for example, GitHub Secrets, AWS Secrets Manager).

  • Rotate keys periodically or immediately if a key is compromised.

  • Use HTTPS for all API requests to prevent key interception.

docs.title: Rate Limits
docs.text:
API keys are subject to rate limiting:

Limit Value Requests/min 100 per API key Reset window 60 seconds Response 429 Too Many Requests

When rate-limited, wait for the reset window before retrying.

docs.title: Change Log
docs.text:
All notable changes to the CRM Order Management API and its documentation are listed here.

Check for recommendations on how to format and structure this file.

This project follows Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
This is the Initial release of the CRM Order Management API.

  • Full CRUD support for orders:

    • POST /orders – Create a new order
    • GET /orders – List all orders with pagination and status filtering
    • GET /orders/{orderId} – Retrieve a single order by ID
    • PUT /orders/{orderId} – Update order fields
    • DELETE /orders/{orderId} – Delete an order

  • Search orders POST /orders/search – Search orders by criteria.

  • Bulk update PATCH /orders/bulk-update – Update status for multiple orders.

  • OpenAPI 3.0.3 specification with full schema definitions, examples, and $ref reuse.

  • API key authentication via X-API-Key header with rate limiting (100 requests/min).

  • Interactive Swagger UI documentation portal.

  • MkDocs Material documentation site with full navigation.

  • GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline: OpenAPI validation → doc generation → MkDocs build → GitHub Pages deployment.

  • Spectral OpenAPI linting ruleset (.spectral.yaml).

  • Python automation script (generate_docs.py) for generating Markdown from the OpenAPI spec.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: The CRM Order Management API uses standard HTTP status codes and returns structured JSON error responses to help you diagnose and resolve issues quickly.

docs.title: Error Response Format
docs.text:
All error responses follow this structure:

{
  "errorCode": "ERROR_CODE",
  "message": "Human-readable description of the error",
  "details": [
    {
      "field": "fieldName",
      "issue": "Description of the validation failure"
    }
  ]
}

The following table describes the fields in the error response.

Field Type Description errorCode string Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling message string Human-readable explanation of what went wrong details array (optional) Per-field validation errors, present on 400 responses

docs.title: Error Codes Reference
docs.text:
The following table lists the possible error codes returned by the API, along with their corresponding HTTP status codes and common causes.

Error Code HTTP Status Cause INVALID_REQUEST 400 One or more required fields are missing or invalid UNAUTHORIZED 401 API key is missing, invalid, or revoked ORDER_NOT_FOUND 404 The specified orderId does not exist CONFLICT 409 Operation not permitted in the order's current status RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED 429 Too many requests sent within the rate limit window INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR 500 An unexpected server error occurred

docs.title: 400 Bad Request
docs.text:
Returned when required fields are missing or field values fail validation.

{
  "errorCode": "INVALID_REQUEST",
  "message": "Missing required fields",
  "details": [
    {
      "field": "customerId",
      "issue": "This field is required"
    },
    {
      "field": "quantity",
      "issue": "Must be greater than zero"
    }
  ]
}

Common causes:

  • Omitting a required field (customerId, productName, quantity, price)
  • Sending quantity as 0 or a negative number
  • Attempting to update an order with status DELIVERED or CANCELLED

docs.title: 401 Unauthorized
docs.text:
Returned when the X-API-Key header is missing or the key is not valid.

{
  "errorCode": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "message": "Invalid or missing API key"
}

Resolution: Ensure the X-API-Key header is present in your request and the key is active. Contact the support team if you need a new key.

docs.title: 404 Not Found
docs.text:
Returned when the requested orderId does not exist in the system.

{
  "errorCode": "ORDER_NOT_FOUND",
  "message": "Order with ID ORD999 was not found"
}

Resolution: Verify the orderId value. Use GET /orders to retrieve a list of valid order IDs.

docs.title: 409 Conflict
docs.text:
Returned when an operation is not permitted given the order's current status.

{
  "errorCode": "CONFLICT",
  "message": "Cannot delete an order with status SHIPPED"
}

Common causes:

  • Attempting to delete an order with status SHIPPED, PROCESSING, or DELIVERED

  • Attempting to update a DELIVERED or CANCELLED order

Resolution: Check the Order Lifecycle to understand valid status transitions.

docs.title: 429 Too Many Requests
docs.text:
Returned when the API key exceeds the rate limit of 100 requests per minute.

{
  "errorCode": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
  "message": "Too many requests. Please retry after 60 seconds."
}

Resolution: Wait for the 60-second reset window before retrying. Implement exponential backoff in your integration to avoid hitting rate limits.

docs.title: 500 Internal Server Error
docs.text:
Returned when an unexpected error occurs on the server side.

{
  "errorCode": "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR",
  "message": "An unexpected error occurred. Please contact support."
}

Resolution: If this error persists, contact the support team with the timestamp and your request details.

docs.title: Handling Errors in Code
docs.text: The following examples demonstrate how to handle API errors in Python and JavaScript.

docs.title: Python
docs.text:
import requests

response = requests.get(
    "https://api.example.com/v1/orders/ORD999",
    headers={"X-API-Key": "your-api-key-here"}
)

if response.status_code == 200:
    order = response.json()
elif response.status_code == 404:
    error = response.json()
    print(f"Order not found: {error['message']}")
elif response.status_code == 401:
    print("Authentication failed. Check your API key.")
else:
    print(f"Unexpected error {response.status_code}: {response.text}")

docs.title: JavaScript
docs.text:
const response = await fetch("https://api.example.com/v1/orders/ORD999", {
  headers: { "X-API-Key": "your-api-key-here" }
});

if (!response.ok) {
  const error = await response.json();
  console.error(`Error ${response.status}: ${error.message}`);
} else {
  const order = await response.json();
  console.log(order);
}

docs.title: CRM Order Management API
docs.text:
Auto-generated from docs/openapi/v1/openapi.yaml on 2026-04-14 16:53 UTC. Do not edit this file directly – update the OpenAPI spec and re-run generate_docs.py.

The CRM Order Management API enables developers to create, retrieve, update, and delete customer orders within a CRM system.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: All endpoints require an API key passed in the X-API-Key header.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: https://api.example.com/v1

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text: Requests are limited to 100 per minute per API key. Exceeding this limit returns a 429 Too Many Requests response.

docs.title: Base URLs
docs.text:
• https://api.example.com/v1 – Production server
  • http://localhost:8000/v1 – Local development server (default: port 8000)

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Type: API Key Header: X-API-Key (in header) Description: API key for authentication. Contact support team to obtain a key.

Example:

curl -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key" https://api.example.com/v1/orders

docs.title: Parameter Reference
docs.text:
The following table lists all fields used in the Order Management API.

Parameter Type Description Required orderId string Unique identifier for the order. Auto-generated by the system. Yes (for specific operations) customerId string Unique identifier for the customer placing the order. Yes productName string Name of the product being ordered. Yes quantity integer Number of units ordered. Must be greater than zero. Yes price number (float) Unit price of the product Yes status string Current lifecycle status of the order. Yes createdAt string (date-time) Timestamp when the order was created. No (system-set) updatedAt string (date-time) Timestamp when the order was last updated. No (system-set)

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: Method Endpoint Summary POST /orders Create a new order GET /orders List all orders GET /orders/{orderId} Get an order by ID PUT /orders/{orderId} Update an order DELETE /orders/{orderId} Delete an order POST /orders/search Search orders with advanced filters PATCH /orders/bulk-update Update status for multiple orders

docs.title: POST /orders
docs.text:
Summary: Create a new order

Creates a new order in the CRM system. The orderId is auto-generated and returned in the response. The initial status is set to CREATED.

Operation ID: createOrder

Request Body:

{
  "customerId": "CUST122",
  "productName": "Laptop",
  "quantity": 1,
  "price": 75000,
  "status": "CREATED"
}

Response – 201 Order created successfully.:

{
  "orderId": "ORD116",
  "customerId": "CUST122",
  "productName": "Laptop",
  "quantity": 1,
  "price": 75000,
  "status": "CREATED",
  "createdAt": "2026-04-08T10:30:00Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-04-08T10:30:00Z"
}

Error Responses:

400 Invalid input. Required fields are missing or values are invalid.

{
  "errorCode": "INVALID_REQUEST",
  "message": "Missing required fields",
  "details": [
    {
      "field": "customerId",
      "issue": "This field is required"
    }
  ]
}

401 Missing or invalid API key.

{
  "errorCode": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "message": "Invalid or missing API key"
}

429 Rate limit exceeded.

{
  "errorCode": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
  "message": "Too many requests. Please retry after 60 seconds."
}

500 An unexpected error occurred on the server.

{
  "errorCode": "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR",
  "message": "An unexpected error occurred. Please contact support."
}

docs.title: GET /orders
docs.text:
Summary: List all orders

Retrieves a paginated list of all orders. Use the status query parameter to filter orders by their lifecycle status.

Operation ID: listOrders

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description status string No – Filter orders by status. page integer No 1 Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1. pageSize integer No 20 Number of results per page. Defaults to 20, maximum 100.

Response – 200 Paginated list of orders.:

{
  "orders": [
    {
      "orderId": "ORD115",
      "customerId": "CUST121",
      "productName": "Acoustic Guitar",
      "quantity": 1,
      "price": 7099.0,
      "status": "SHIPPED",
      "createdAt": "2026-04-07T09:15:00Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-04-08T12:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 1,
  "page": 1,
  "pageSize": 20
}

Error Responses:

401 Missing or invalid API key.

{
  "errorCode": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "message": "Invalid or missing API key"
}

429 Rate limit exceeded.

{
  "errorCode": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
  "message": "Too many requests. Please retry after 60 seconds."
}

500 An unexpected error occurred on the server.

{
  "errorCode": "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR",
  "message": "An unexpected error occurred. Please contact support."
}

docs.title: GET /orders/{orderId}
docs.text:
Summary: Get an order by ID

Retrieves the full details of a specific order by its unique orderId.

Operation ID: getOrderById

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description orderId string Yes Unique identifier of the order.

Response – 200 Order details retrieved successfully.:

{
  "orderId": "ORD115",
  "customerId": "CUST121",
  "productName": "Acoustic Guitar",
  "quantity": 1,
  "price": 7099.0,
  "status": "DELIVERED",
  "createdAt": "2026-04-07T09:15:00Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-04-10T16:00:00Z"
}

Error Responses:

401 Missing or invalid API key.

{
  "errorCode": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "message": "Invalid or missing API key"
}

404 Order not found. The specified orderId does not exist.

{
  "errorCode": "ORDER_NOT_FOUND",
  "message": "Order with ID ORD115 was not found"
}

429 Rate limit exceeded.

{
  "errorCode": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
  "message": "Too many requests. Please retry after 60 seconds."
}

500 An unexpected error occurred on the server.

{
  "errorCode": "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR",
  "message": "An unexpected error occurred. Please contact support."
}

docs.title: PUT /orders/{orderId}
docs.text:
Summary: Update an order

Updates one or more fields of an existing order. Only the fields included in the request body are updated; all other fields remain unchanged.

Note: Orders with status DELIVERED or CANCELLED cannot be updated.

Operation ID: updateOrder

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description orderId string Yes Unique identifier of the order.

Request Body:

{
  "status": "SHIPPED"
}

Response – 200 Order updated successfully.:

{
  "orderId": "ORD115",
  "customerId": "CUST121",
  "productName": "Acoustic Guitar",
  "quantity": 1,
  "price": 7099.0,
  "status": "SHIPPED",
  "createdAt": "2026-04-07T09:15:00Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-04-10T14:00:00Z"
}

Error Responses:

400 Invalid update data.

{
  "errorCode": "INVALID_REQUEST",
  "message": "Cannot update a DELIVERED order"
}

401 Missing or invalid API key.

{
  "errorCode": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "message": "Invalid or missing API key"
}

404 Order not found.

{
  "errorCode": "ORDER_NOT_FOUND",
  "message": "Order with ID ORD115 was not found"
}

429 Rate limit exceeded.

{
  "errorCode": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
  "message": "Too many requests. Please retry after 60 seconds."
}

500 An unexpected error occurred on the server.

{
  "errorCode": "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR",
  "message": "An unexpected error occurred. Please contact support."
}

docs.title: DELETE /orders/{orderId}
docs.text:
Summary: Delete an order

Permanently deletes an order from the system. This action is irreversible.

Note: Only orders with status CREATED or CANCELLED can be deleted.

Operation ID: deleteOrder

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description orderId string Yes Unique identifier of the order.

Response – 204 Order deleted successfully. No content is returned.:

Order deleted successfully. No content is returned.. No response body.

Error Responses:

401 Missing or invalid API key.

{
  "errorCode": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "message": "Invalid or missing API key"
}

404 Order not found.

{
  "errorCode": "ORDER_NOT_FOUND",
  "message": "Order with ID ORD115 was not found"
}

409 Conflict. Order cannot be deleted in its current status.

{
  "errorCode": "CONFLICT",
  "message": "Cannot delete an order with status SHIPPED"
}

429 Rate limit exceeded.

{
  "errorCode": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
  "message": "Too many requests. Please retry after 60 seconds."
}

500 An unexpected error occurred on the server.

{
  "errorCode": "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR",
  "message": "An unexpected error occurred. Please contact support."
}

docs.title: POST /orders/search
docs.text:
Summary: Search orders with advanced filters

Search for orders using flexible filter criteria including status, customer ID, and date ranges. Returns a paginated result set. Useful for building dashboards and reports.

Operation ID: searchOrders

Request Body:

{
  "filters": {
    "status": "SHIPPED",
    "customerId": "CUST121",
    "dateRange": {
      "from": "2026-04-01T00:00:00Z",
      "to": "2026-04-14T23:59:59Z"
    }
  },
  "pagination": {
    "page": 1,
    "pageSize": 50
  }
}

Response – 200 Search results retrieved successfully:

{
  "data": [
    {
      "orderId": "ORD115",
      "customerId": "CUST121",
      "productName": "Acoustic Guitar",
      "quantity": 1,
      "price": 7099.0,
      "status": "SHIPPED",
      "createdAt": "2026-04-08T14:32:00Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-04-10T09:15:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "page": 1,
    "pageSize": 50,
    "total": 235,
    "totalPages": 5
  }
}

Error Responses:

400 Invalid filter parameters

{
  "errorCode": "INVALID_REQUEST",
  "message": "Invalid date range format"
}

401 Missing or invalid API key.

{
  "errorCode": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "message": "Invalid or missing API key"
}

429 Rate limit exceeded.

{
  "errorCode": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
  "message": "Too many requests. Please retry after 60 seconds."
}

500 An unexpected error occurred on the server.

{
  "errorCode": "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR",
  "message": "An unexpected error occurred. Please contact support."
}

docs.title: PATCH /orders/bulk-update
docs.text:
Summary: Update status for multiple orders

Update the status of multiple orders in a single request. Useful for bulk operations on shipped batches or batch processing. Maximum 100 orders per request.

Operation ID: bulkUpdateOrders

Request Body:

{
  "orderIds": [
    "ORD115",
    "ORD116",
    "ORD117"
  ],
  "status": "SHIPPED"
}

Response – 200 Orders updated successfully. Returns summary of successes and failures.:

{
  "updated": 98,
  "failed": 2,
  "failures": [
    {
      "orderId": "ORD999",
      "reason": "Order not found"
    },
    {
      "orderId": "ORD1000",
      "reason": "Cannot transition from DELIVERED to PROCESSING"
    }
  ]
}

Error Responses:

400 Invalid request body or parameters

{
  "errorCode": "INVALID_REQUEST",
  "message": "orderIds array cannot be empty"
}

401 Missing or invalid API key.

{
  "errorCode": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "message": "Invalid or missing API key"
}

429 Rate limit exceeded.

{
  "errorCode": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
  "message": "Too many requests. Please retry after 60 seconds."
}

500 An unexpected error occurred on the server.

{
  "errorCode": "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR",
  "message": "An unexpected error occurred. Please contact support."
}

docs.title: HTTP Status Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning Description 200 OK Successful GET or PUT. 201 Created Resource created successfully via POST. 204 No Content Resource deleted successfully via DELETE. 400 Bad Request Missing required fields or invalid values. 401 Unauthorized API key is missing, invalid, or revoked. 404 Not Found The specified resource does not exist. 409 Conflict Operation not allowed in the resource's current state. 429 Too Many Requests Rate limit exceeded. Retry after 60 seconds. 500 Internal Server Error Unexpected server error. Contact support.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks you through setting up and making your first API call to the CRM Order Management API.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, ensure that you have the following:

  • An API key (see Authentication)

  • curl or Postman installed

  • Basic knowledge of REST APIs and JSON

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text:
All API requests use the following base URL:

https://api.example.com/v1

For local development:

http://localhost:8000/v1

Following are the steps to get started with the API:

  • Obtain an API key
  • Make your first API call
  • Create a new order
  • Explore the Interactive Reference
  • Try advanced examples

docs.title: Obtain an API Key
docs.text:
All endpoints require authentication via an API key. Contact the support team to request a key.

Once issued, include your key in every request header:

X-API-Key: your-api-key-here

For more details, see Authentication.

docs.title: List all orders
docs.text:
Use the GET /orders endpoint to retrieve a list of orders:

curl -X GET "https://api.example.com/v1/orders" \
  -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key-here" \
  -H "Accept: application/json"

Expected response:

{
  "orders": [
    {
      "orderId": "ORD115",
      "customerId": "CUST121",
      "productName": "Acoustic Guitar",
      "quantity": 1,
      "price": 7099.0,
      "status": "SHIPPED",
      "createdAt": "2026-04-07T09:15:00Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-04-08T12:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 1,
  "page": 1,
  "pageSize": 20
}

docs.title: Create a New Order
docs.text:
Use the POST /orders endpoint to create a new order:

curl -X POST "https://api.example.com/v1/orders" \
  -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key-here" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "customerId": "CUST122",
    "productName": "Laptop",
    "quantity": 1,
    "price": 75000
  }'

Expected response (HTTP 201):

{
  "orderId": "ORD116",
  "customerId": "CUST122",
  "productName": "Laptop",
  "quantity": 1,
  "price": 75000,
  "status": "CREATED",
  "createdAt": "2026-04-08T10:30:00Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-04-08T10:30:00Z"
}

docs.title: Explore the Interactive Reference
docs.text: Use the Swagger UI to browse all endpoints interactively, try out requests, and inspect request and response schemas directly in your browser – no additional tooling required.

docs.title: Advanced Examples
docs.text: Use the API to perform complex operations like filtering orders, bulk updates, and handling errors. See the CRM Order Management API for detailed examples and best practices.

docs.title: Filter Orders by Status
docs.text:
List only shipped orders with pagination:

curl -X GET "https://api.example.com/v1/orders?status=SHIPPED&page=1&pageSize=50" \
  -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key-here"

Query parameters:

  • status – Filter by order status (CREATED, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED)
  • page – Page number (default: 1)
  • pageSize – Results per page (default: 20, maximum: 100)

docs.title: Search Orders with Advanced Filters
docs.text:
Find orders for a specific customer within a date range:

curl -X POST "https://api.example.com/v1/orders/search" \
  -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key-here" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "filters": {
      "customerId": "CUST121",
      "status": "SHIPPED",
      "dateRange": {
        "from": "2026-04-01T00:00:00Z",
        "to": "2026-04-14T23:59:59Z"
      }
    },
    "pagination": {
      "page": 1,
      "pageSize": 25
    }
  }'

Response:

{
  "data": [
    {
      "orderId": "ORD115",
      "customerId": "CUST121",
      "productName": "Acoustic Guitar",
      "quantity": 1,
      "price": 7099.0,
      "status": "SHIPPED",
      "createdAt": "2026-04-08T14:32:00Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-04-10T09:15:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "page": 1,
    "pageSize": 25,
    "total": 235,
    "totalPages": 10
  }
}

docs.title: Bulk Update Order Status
docs.text:
Update the status of multiple orders in one request:

curl -X PATCH "https://api.example.com/v1/orders/bulk-update" \
  -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key-here" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "orderIds": ["ORD115", "ORD116", "ORD117"],
    "status": "SHIPPED"
  }'

Response:

{
  "updated": 3,
  "failed": 0,
  "failures": []
}

If some orders fail to update:

{
  "updated": 2,
  "failed": 1,
  "failures": [
    {
      "orderId": "ORD117",
      "reason": "Cannot transition from DELIVERED to SHIPPED"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Update a Single Order
docs.text:
Modify order fields (status, quantity, price):

curl -X PUT "https://api.example.com/v1/orders/ORD115" \
  -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key-here" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "status": "SHIPPED",
    "quantity": 2
  }'

Response (HTTP 200):

{
  "orderId": "ORD115",
  "customerId": "CUST121",
  "productName": "Acoustic Guitar",
  "quantity": 2,
  "price": 7099.0,
  "status": "SHIPPED",
  "createdAt": "2026-04-07T09:15:00Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-04-14T10:15:00Z"
}

docs.title: Handle API Errors
docs.text:
If a request fails, the API returns a structured error response:

curl -X POST "https://api.example.com/v1/orders" \
  -H "X-API-Key: invalid-key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"customerId": "CUST122"}'

Response (HTTP 401):

{
  "errorCode": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "message": "Invalid or missing API key"
}

Response (HTTP 400 - validation error):

{
  "errorCode": "INVALID_REQUEST",
  "message": "Missing required fields",
  "details": [
    {
      "field": "productName",
      "issue": "This field is required"
    },
    {
      "field": "quantity",
      "issue": "This field is required"
    }
  ]
}

For more error scenarios, see Error Handling.

docs.title: Running the Documentation Locally
docs.text:
To run this documentation site on your local machine:

  1. Clone the repository

    git clone https://github.com/your-username/api-doc-automation.git
cd api-doc-automation

  2. Install dependencies

    pip install mkdocs-material pyyaml

  3. Generate API docs from the OpenAPI spec

    python scripts/generate_docs.py

  4. Start the local docs server

    mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000 in your browser.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Read the full CRM Order Management API
  • Understand Authentication
  • Learn about Error Handling

docs.title: Interactive API Reference
docs.text:
The interactive API reference is powered by Swagger UI and rendered directly from the OpenAPI 3.0 specification.

Use it to:

  • Browse all available endpoints
  • View request and response schemas
  • Try out live API calls directly from your browser (add your API key in the Authorize dialog)

docs.title: Open the Interactive Reference
docs.text: Open Swagger UI

docs.title: Using Swagger UI
docs.text:
1. Click Authorize in the top right and enter your X-API-Key.
  2. Select an endpoint to expand it.
  3. Click Try it out, fill in any parameters, and click Execute.
  4. View the response body, status code, and headers.

docs.title: Download the OpenAPI Spec
docs.text:
The raw OpenAPI 3.0 specification is available at:

openapi/v1/openapi.yaml

You can import this file into Postman or any OpenAPI-compatible tooling.
